text: This is the VOA Special English Agriculture
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语农业报道。

text: Report. Soil naturally contains harmless amounts of lead along with other metals.
zh: [1] 土壤中天然含有无害量的铅以及其他金属。

text: Because of pollution, however, the amounts are higher the closer you get to cities and towns.
zh: [2] 但由于污染的原因,离城镇越近,铅的含量就越高。

text: But experts say this should not stop gardeners from growing food if they take safety measures.
zh: [3] 不过专家表示,只要采取安全措施,这不应阻止园艺爱好者种植食物。

text: David Johnson is a chemistry professor
zh: [4] 戴维·约翰逊是一位化学教授,

text: in the State University of New York's College of Environmental Science and Forestry.
zh: [5] 任职于纽约州立大学环境科学与林业学院。

text: He explains that lead can enter the ground
zh: [6] 他解释说,铅可以通过多种途径进入土壤,

text: from leaded paint and leaded fuel and from industry.
zh: [7] 比如含铅涂料、含铅燃料以及工业排放。

text: And once lead gets into the soil, it stays a long time.
zh: [8] 铅一旦进入土壤,就会长期存在。

text: It remains an environmental threat, especially to children.
zh: [9] 它仍是一种环境威胁,对儿童尤其如此。

text: Lead can harm mental and physical development even in babies before they are born.
zh: [10] 铅甚至会在婴儿出生前就损害其智力和身体发育。

text: If a test finds that soil has too much lead,
zh: [11] 如果检测发现土壤中铅含量过高,

text: you might be advised to remove the soil or cover it with sod grass.
zh: [12] 你可能会被建议清除这些土壤或用草皮覆盖。

text: Different countries have different levels that they consider acceptable.
zh: [13] 不同国家对可接受铅含量的标准也不同。

text: Carl Rosen of the University of Minnesota Extension notes
zh: [14] 明尼苏达大学推广项目的卡尔·罗森指出,

text: that concerns about lead have increased now that more people are planting gardens.
zh: [15] 随着越来越多的人开始种植花园,人们对铅污染的担忧也在增加。

text: But Professor Rosen says plants generally do not absorb much lead.
zh: [16] 但罗森教授表示,植物通常不会吸收太多铅。

text: He says there is likely more risk from lead dust on plants
zh: [17] 他说,风险更可能来自附着在植物上的铅尘,

text: or from playing in the soil than from the plant itself.
zh: [18] 或来自在土壤中玩耍,而非植物本身。

text: Still, some plants do absorb more than others.
zh: [19] 尽管如此,有些植物确实比其他植物吸收更多铅。

text: Experts say good choices for the garden
zh: [20] 专家表示,适合种植的作物

text: include tomatoes, peppers, beans, and okra.
zh: [21] 包括西红柿、辣椒、豆类和秋葵。

text: Among the plants that can absorb more More contaminants,
zh: [22] 而在能吸收更多污染物的植物中,

text: they say, are root crops, leafy vegetables, and herbs.
zh: [23] 专家指出,包括根茎类作物、叶菜和香草。

text: If you grow carrots, for example, you might want to peel them.
zh: [24] 例如,如果你种胡萝卜,可能需要削皮。

text: Dirt may be harder to remove from some crops than from others. But all produce should be washed.
zh: [25] 有些作物比其他作物更难清除泥土,但所有农产品都应清洗。

text: Gardens should be planted away from roads and structures, especially old buildings.
zh: [26] 花园应远离道路和建筑物,尤其是老旧建筑。

text: home gardeners should plant away from the foundation of their house
zh: [27] 家庭园丁应避免在房屋地基附近种植,

text: and lead amounts can be especially high near industrial areas and waste dumps
zh: [28] 而在工业区和垃圾场附近,铅含量可能特别高。

text: soil should also be tested for its pH level a measure of the acid and alkaline balance
zh: [29] 土壤还应检测其pH值,即酸碱平衡的指标。

text: experts say the ability to take up lead lead is reduced when the pH level is above 6 .5.
zh: [30] 专家表示,当pH值高于6.5时,植物吸收铅的能力会降低。

text: Adding organic materials such as compost to soil can also make lead less available to plants.
zh: [31] 在土壤中添加堆肥等有机物质,也能减少植物对铅的吸收。

text: Some people attempt a process called phytoremediation.
zh: [32] 有些人尝试一种叫做植物修复的方法。

text: They try to remove lead from soil by growing certain plants that collect it. it.
zh: [33] 他们试图通过种植某些能吸收铅的植物来清除土壤中的铅。

text: But Professor Rosen says the process is complex and may not work.
zh: [34] 但罗森教授说,这个过程很复杂,可能并不奏效。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [35] 以上就是美国之音特别英语农业报道。

text: For more gardening information, go to voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: [36] 想了解更多园艺信息,请访问voaspecialenglish.com。
